title: Define and ship the sidecar cache schema (DDL + version metadata)
description: Formalize the SQLite schema for autocue_cache.sqlite. Schema is created inside TASK-010's _init_schema, but this task pins the DDL, columns, indexes, and version metadata so reviewers can sign off on the storage layout independently of the wiring code.
acceptanceCriteria: Schema file (or constant string in autocue/cache.py) contains all 6 CREATE TABLE statements and 2 CREATE INDEX statements exactly as specified in the PRD §7.
Schema version stored in meta(key='schema_version', value='1').
All floating-point fields stored as REAL.
BLOB packing format is documented inline (struct format strings).
Schema is documented in CLAUDE.md under the sidecar cache section.
A schema-dump utility (autocue/cache.py:dump_schema(path) -> str) returns the live CREATE statements for diffing.

steps.details: In autocue/cache.py define a module-level constant SCHEMA_DDL: list[str] containing each CREATE TABLE / CREATE INDEX statement verbatim. Tables: meta, energy_curve, classification, similarity_vector, mixability, tracks_snapshot. Indexes: idx_energy_mtime, idx_class_mtime. _init_schema iterates SCHEMA_DDL.

ENERGY_CURVE_PACK_FORMAT = lambda n: f'{n}f'. SIMILARITY_VECTOR_PACK_FORMAT = '6f'. Inline comments in cache.py explain that float32 (NOT float64) is used to keep blob sizes small (~200 bytes per energy curve at n_points=50).
dump_schema(cache_path) opens the file, runs SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type IN ('table','index'), returns concatenated SQL. Used by tests and for ops.
Add a 'sidecar cache schema' subsection under 'Must-know constraints' (or in a depth file under .claude/project/). Include the DDL block and the BLOB format note.

tests/test_cache.py: test_schema_dump_matches_ddl(): open a fresh CacheStore; dump_schema; normalize whitespace; assert each SCHEMA_DDL entry is a substring. Future schema bumps will fail this test until SCHEMA_DDL is updated.

technicalNotes: No migrations in v1. If SCHEMA_VERSION must bump, the open path drops + recreates all tables. Cache is regenerable from ANLZ files, so user impact is one-time warm-up.
SQLite TEXT/INTEGER/REAL types are dynamic - column types are advisory. Keep them anyway for readability and for tools that introspect schema.
